original_abstract: The rapid maturation of deep learning has catalyzed the development of sophisticated generative models capable of addressing the inherent limitations of real-world time series datasets. Deep neural networks are notoriously data-hungry, yet time series data in practical applications—ranging from finance to healthcare—are often characterized by scarcity, class imbalance, and strict privacy constraints. To mitigate these challenges, data augmentation (DA) has emerged as a vital strategy for expanding training sets and enhancing model robustness. This paper provides a comprehensive survey and a novel taxonomy of the current state of the art in time series data augmentation. We categorize methodologies into traditional transformation-based techniques, such as jittering, scaling, and permutation, and advanced generative frameworks, including Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs). Furthermore, we review specialized alignment-based methods like Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) barycenter averaging. The survey critically evaluates the specific strengths and weaknesses of each approach, discusses the complexities of performance metrics and evaluation strategies, and identifies persistent challenges such as training instability and the preservation of temporal dependencies. By synthesizing the evolution of the field and categorizing the most effective algorithms, this study serves as a foundational guide for researchers seeking to optimize lead-time and accuracy in time series modeling through synthetic data enrichment.
humanized_abstract: Deep learning is a relatively new field of research that has led to the development of generative models as an alternative solution for the scarcity of time series data in real life applications. Deep learning models require massive amount of data in order to be trained. Time series data from real life applications as financial and clinical time series suffer from the data scarcity problem, in addition to the class imbalance and high sensitivity to noise issues. Hence, data augmentation (DA) has become a very important technique to assist the deep learning models by generating sufficient training data to improve the performance of the model and to enhance the robustness against different types of noise. In this paper, we provide a detailed survey on the recent works in time series data augmentation by introducing a taxonomy that classifies the augmentation methods into two main subcategories: Transformation based methods, such as jittering, scaling and permutation, and Generative models including Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) and Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), in addition to Alignment-based methods like Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) barycenter averaging. We also analyzed each method highlighting the advantages and drawbacks of each approach, in addition to discussing the difficulties and challenges facing the data augmentation research field, such as the instability of the GANs, as well as providing the performance evaluation and the corresponding metrics that are used in the field of time series data augmentation. Our ultimate goal is to summarize the state of the art of time series data augmentation in an attempt to recommend to the time series modeling community the most efficient data augmentation techniques in order to increase the lead time accuracy and to utilize synthetic data in enriching the real data.
